entry.title: Creating a story with Dymanic Version & Date Combinations
entry.content:
Introduction

 

Designing a story in SAP Analytics Cloud can be easy and straightforward with many features to create calculations, columns, styles. The drag and drop functionality helps users create reports with different layouts of various complexity . Optimized story design is also helpful to create advanced stories and reporting layouts using JavaScript to achieve complex functionality and user interactions.

When designing any report or input form, if the layout contains Version and Date dimensions, adding multiple members of versions and Months or Years can result in a Layout that has columns with all the combinations of the members. For example, if the selections contain Actual, Budget for Version and Date contains years 2024 and 2025, the report will have 4 columns. Even though the intention is to show only 2 columns with combinations of [Actual, 2023] and [Budget, 2024], the final report contains all 4 combinations.

There are ways in which we can show only valid/required combinations, for example hiding unwanted columns or suppress columns with no data or using Advanced Filters. But these approaches are not dynamic. Every time there is a change in Version or Date, the columns need to be adjusted manually. It might also create many static reports to achieve different combination reports.

The alternate approach is to create dynamic reports is using Optimized story design using JavaScript and SAC Advanced Script API to show columns with only valid/required combinations. This blog provides required design and logic required for interactions and filtering.
 

Step 1 - Creating Design Elements

The design of the dashboard uses dropdown menus instead of filters define within Story. The user can select required Dimension members from dropdown and on submit the selections will be sent to the table filters dynamically. We will use story calculations to create required combinations and further use SAC Analytics designer and API to update dimension filters with required combinations.

Step 1-a
  • Create a New story using Optimized Story design.
  • Add 4 Dropdown menus ( Insert -> Filters/Controls -> Dropdown). 2 for Actual / 2023 and 2 for Budget/ 2024. 
  • Dropdown names dd_fy1, dd_fy2 for years & dd_cat1, dd_cat2 for versions. 
  • Add 1 submit button ( Insert -> Button )
  • Button name : btn_1
  • Add 1 Table and connect to model. Add necessary filters. ( We need to update the filters at a later stage to show necessary columns)
  • Table Name: table_1

 

Step 1-b
Once the dropdown menus are added, from the script edit page, add the values required for the dropdown menus. Year dropdown has values as Years and Version dropdown has values as Version names. Below images show the options used for each dropdown.
 
 
 
 
The final result will be 4 dropdown menus with values and a Submit button. 
 

 

Step 2 - Logic 

Till now we have added design elements in the Story. Now we need to add logic to capture the values selected from dropdown menus and send them story filters.

logic to be added on btn_1 onClick function

The logic has 3 main parts. 

  1. extract the selections used from the dropdown menus and pass them to variables and arrays
  2. create valid combinations by combining Version + year values and push into array
  3. Filter the Version, Year and Valid Combinations values on the table

 

 

 

//extract selected versions into variables 
var version1 = dd_cat1.getSelectedKey(); 
var version2 = dd_cat2.getSelectedKey(); 

// append public to variables created above 
var col1_version = "public."+version1; 
var col2_version = "public."+version2; 

//extract selected versions into variables 
var col1_fy = dd_fy1.getSelectedKey(); 
var col2_fy = dd_fy2.getSelectedKey(); 

// save table into variable 
var ds_table1 = table_1.getDataSource(); 

// get dimension names into variables. Note the order of dimensions added in columns. 
// 0 is version, 1 is for year 
var version_col =table_1.getDimensionsOnColumns()[0]; 
var fy_col =table_1.getDimensionsOnColumns()[1]; 

// create an empty array to hold version values and add the version variables created above 
var version_cols_values = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string); 
version_cols_values.push(col1_version); 
version_cols_values.push(col2_version); 

// create an empty array to hold year values and add the year variables created above 
var fy_cols_values = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string); 
fy_cols_values.push(col1_fy); 
fy_cols_values.push(col2_fy); 

// create an empty array for valid combinations 
// for example Actual2023, Budget2024 
var valid_comb = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string); 
valid_comb.push(version1+col1_fy); 
valid_comb.push(version2+col2_fy); 


// set the 3 dimension filters for Version, Date, valid_combinations on the table 
ds_table1.setDimensionFilter(version_col,version_cols_values); 
ds_table1.setDimensionFilter(fy_col,fy_cols_values); 

ds_table1.setDimensionFilter(table_1.getDimensionsOnColumns()[2],valid_comb);

 

 

 

Step 3 - Story

The JavaScript logic above filters the dimension values for Version and Time , Fiscal Year values. But filtering alone on Version and Time still result in additional columns which are not required. So we need to create an additional calculated dimension call "valid_combinations" to identify the required columns and filter them during the runtime.

 

 

Below is the code used for creating the calculated dimension. The code uses Version and Fiscal Year to derive the all the possible combinations of columns. One can add many versions, years and create all past and future values possible.

 

 

 

IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2022"),
        "Actual2022",
        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2023"),
                "Actual2023",
                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2024"),
                        "Actual2024",
                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2025"),
                                "Actual2025",
                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2022"),
                                        "Budget2022",
                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2023"),
                                                "Budget2023",
                                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2024"),
                                                        "Budget2024",
                                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2025"),
                                                                "Budget2025",
                                                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2022"),
                                                                        "Plan2022",
                                                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2023"),
                                                                                "Plan2023",
                                                                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2024"),
                                                                                        "Plan2024",
                                                                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2025"),
                                                                                                "Plan2025",
                                                                                                "Actual2025"
                                                                                        )
                                                                                )
                                                                        )
                                                                )
                                                        )
                                                )
                                        )
                                )
                        )
                )
        )
)

 

 

 

Result

The final result will contain table with only required columns.

entry.title: Costing variant PPC4 is not available in configuration

entry.title: Re: Costing variant PPC4 is not available in configuration
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


There is no specific app/activity to configure Sales Costing Variant PPC4, but you can check the parameters assigned to the related Valuation Variant (Costing Variant PPC4 is assigned with Valuation Variant 001).


To configure Valuation Variants, you can use Configuration Activity / SSCUI "Define Valuation Variants per Plant".


You can also refer to the below KBA:

3350673 - How to check Costing/Valuation Variants for Sales Costing


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Error 'First create the valuation-type-independent da...

On the tab  "Valuation data" in the Product Template, you have to define one valuation header record with specified valuation category and initial valuation type and one dependent record for each valuation type.

 

Please check sample for Split Valuation available at the migration object Product Documentation

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Convert Date from OData
entry.content:
Hello team,

 

Just for reference for other community members,

 

I have used below javascript code to convert the date to oData date format. 

I have fired the event once the date field is changed to call the Javascript and store it into different variable. 

const inputDateTime = inputs.input1;
const date = new Date(inputDateTime);;

// Step 2: Set the time to the start of the day
date.setUTCHours(0, 0, 0, 0);
// Step 3: Format the resulting date to the desired string format
const year = date.getUTCFullYear();
// because there is no less equal to operator in oData supported by JAVASCRIPT so need to -1 in From Date.
date.setUTCDate(date.getUTCDate() - 1);

const month = String(date.getUTCMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0'); // Months are zero-based
const day = String(date.getUTCDate()).padStart(2, '0');
const hours = String(date.getUTCHours()).padStart(2, '0');
const minutes = String(date.getUTCMinutes()).padStart(2, '0');
const seconds = String(date.getUTCSeconds()).padStart(2, '0');

var result1 = year + '-' + month + '-' + day + 'T' + hours + ':' + minutes + ':' + seconds ;
return{ result : result1 };

entry.title: Analyze License Consumption Consolidated Data with the SAM4U Solution from SAP
entry.content:
Are you still paying for license analysis consultancy services that use their proprietary tools, marketed as miracle solutions? Forget about it! Now you can leverage an SAP solution that offers beautifully designed Fiori views to analyze user, engine, and even HANA Enterprise Edition license consumption.

Introducing SAM4U: The SAP Solution for License Analysis

Meet SAM4U, the innovative solution designed by SAP to streamline your license analysis. You can explore the demo version right from our website.

Today’s post is about consolidated data analysis and how you can create a consolidation corresponding to your measurement plan in just a few minutes.

When you install the SAM4U extractor to your backend system (read more about it in our previous blogs), you can start piping data to SAM4U, for instance, via a ZIPped file.

Pic. 01. Uploading ZIP File Produced by SAM4U Extractor

If you're uploading a file for the first time, it will also create system metadata from the extracted dataset. Each subsequent upload is grouped at the system level, allowing you to drill down and see all datasets you’ve uploaded:

 Pic. 02. Datasets Overview for a Certain System

Once you’ve uploaded data for all systems for your consolidation, navigate to the “Consolidation” view. Click the create button to join all the systems you’d like to consolidate. You can even add HANA EE systems, ensuring that HANA EE license consumption KPIs in the SAM4U tool are also populated:

Pic. 03. Creation of Consolidation

That’s it! You’ve done it yourself 😊 Now SAM4U KPIs are filled, and you can enjoy the detailed analysis of your license consumption. In our example, we noticed we're using more licenses than we're entitled to 😉

Pic. 04. Users’ Licenses Consumption Analysis

Install and enjoy the SAM4U tool, or get it hosted by us to keep your costs under control.

P.S. Check out our entire blog series about the SAM4U tool at this link: SAM4U as a Service.

entry.title: Re: RISE, with embedded set up
entry.content:
Hello Raj,

Please check your GRC deployment model as per SAP Note 2229853 - GRC and S/4HANA oP: compatibility information - SAP for Me. Refresh of GRC non-production is possible only in the side-by-side deployment scenarios. If co-deployment/embedded model is used, then S/4HANA and GRC would be on the same database and it is not possible to refresh just the GRC part using standard refresh procedures.

Thanks, Kishan

entry.title: ABAP RAP: Execute Business Object Interface from action
entry.content:
Hi community,

A common request from customers is that a report execute a certain process and if one of the process "fail" not continue with the others process. In S/4HANA Public Cloud, the replacement for BAPI's are the Local APIs (Business Object Interfaces - BOI) for example I_MaterialDocumentTP. Only for reference, this example is obtained from the standard Knowledge Transfer Document. In theory, the REPORTED structure must return messages in case of an error occurs, but that is not the case.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_materialdocumenttp
             ENTITY MaterialDocument
             CREATE FROM VALUE #( ( %cid                          = 'CID_001'
                                    goodsmovementcode             = '01'
                                    postingdate                   = '31129999'
                                    documentdate                  = '31129999'
                                    %control-goodsmovementcode                    = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                    %control-postingdate                          = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                    %control-documentdate                         = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                ) )
             ENTITY MaterialDocument
             CREATE BY \_MaterialDocumentItem
             FROM VALUE #( (
                             %cid_ref = 'CID_001'
                             %target = VALUE #( ( %cid                           = 'CID_ITM_001'
                                                  plant                          = '1010'
                                                  material                       = 'RAW_MATERIAL_01'
                                                  GoodsMovementType              = '501'
                                                  storagelocation                = '101A'
                                                  QuantityInEntryUnit            = 1
                                                  entryunit                      = 'ST'
                                                  %control-plant                 = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                                  %control-material              = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                                  %control-GoodsMovementType     = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                                  %control-storagelocation       = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                                  %control-QuantityInEntryUnit   = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                                  %control-entryunit             = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed
                                              ) )

                                               
                         ) )
             MAPPED   DATA(ls_create_mapped)
             FAILED   DATA(ls_create_failed)
             REPORTED DATA(ls_create_reported).

 The "errors" only are obtained when the COMMIT ENTITIES in the REPORTED structure.

COMMIT ENTITIES
  RESPONSE OF i_materialdocumenttp
  FAILED DATA(commit_failed)
  REPORTED DATA(commit_reported).

 Of course, in the context of ABAP RAP, the use of COMMIT is not supported.

If I'm developing an application with ABAP RAP and I have an action that need to create a material document and report if an error occurs, who could I achieve that?

I know the existence of different methods of "asynchronous code execution" (app. jobs, bgPF and events), if this is the only way... what could be the best option to report/show to the user that material document is processing in background and when the process ends show the errors or the new document created?

entry.title: APP F110 ERROR (Company codes 557A/557A do not appear in proposal 27.06.2024 ASFG; correct)
Re: Post S/4 2023 upgrade, TM Cockpit does not show truck...
entry.content: Here is SAP response that fixed the problem: The standard association used for the display of the Truck Resource in the page layout is RESOURCE_ACTIVE_ROAD. With this association, we specifically read only those resources having the TRMODCAT as 'Road' alone and the resources having all other modes will be eliminated from getting displayed. If you intend to display other modes as well under this tab you may want to use the association RESOURCE_ACTIVE instead. In standard, for Air mode of transport, we anticipate only the schedule based Freight bookings/Freight order and not AVR based orders.

entry.title: Tolerance check refer to PP （price variance） during invoice verification
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Is there any possibility to check the tolerance of Accumulate Price variance instead of variance of each invoice refer to PP during invoice verification?  for example, we set amount upper threshold is 1000 in OMR6, refer to same PO item, there are two invoices, first invoice exceeds 600, it should be passed without be locked as the tolerance is acceptable, while if the secondary invoice is same, we would like to block the payment as the accumulated tolerance is 1200, over the threshold, so we would like to block the secondary invoice, is it possible? if yes, may we know how? thanks!

PO Line

PO Amount

Invoice Amount

Variance

Current Behavior

Expectation

1

10000

10600

600

Released due to variance is less than 10% and less than 1000 AUD

Released due to variance is less than 10% and less than 1000 AUD

2

10000

10600

600

Released due to variance is less than 10% and less than 1000 AUD

Should block due to variance is greater than 1000 AUD

entry.title: Need Groovy help in achieving below requirement to handle xml data in sap CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 
First let me thank all the people who are help people in this forum which is really helping people like to learn. I have learnt so much from your responses and blogs. Thanks you so much for all your efforts and time to guide people like me.
 
I have the below requirement to achieve in cpi. Please guide/help me to achieve this. I really appreciate your time and energy.
 
Below is the sample payload. I need to first for each <cust_MaximoCalloutRecordDetails> nodes, combine <cust_startDate>, only take date from this field, and take <cust_startTime>, only take time from this field. so first record the vlaue will be 2024-06-25T11:00:00.000. And then sort all the records based this newly combined value from low to high.
 
Then Take the 1st <cust_startTime>, take only Time part in this value, and add 4 hrs to that time and compare it with next records. If any of the next records falls under that 4 hrs time frame, then take <cust_endDate> of that record and <cust_endTime>, only the time part, of that record and the <cust_calloutType> as 0023. <cust_startDate> and <cust_startTime>, only time part, of the first record value.
 
After that, take next record which falls outside of 1st 4 hrs and then again check same logic as above. And then again, take next record which falls outside of 2nd, which falls outof 1st 4 hrs, and repeat the same steps.
 
So every time you create a new record, <cust_calloutType> value varies like for 1st record the vakue will be 1123 and for send record it's 1142 and next record it's 1143 and then next reocrd 1144 and the next record 1145 and the next record 1146
 
Below is the sample payload 
 
<cust_MaximoCalloutRecords>
<cust_MaximoCalloutRecords>
<externalCode>XyZ</externalCode>
        <effectiveStartDate>2024-06-25T00:00:00.000</effectiveStartDate>
<cust_MaximoCalloutRecordDetails1>
<cust_MaximoCalloutRecordDetails>
<cust_startDate>2024-06-25T00:00:00.000</cust_startDate>
<cust_endTime>1970-01-01T11:30:00.000</cust_endTime>

entry.title: SAP HANA Topology match for System Copy
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are planning to execute system copy of our SAP PERSON_NAME system from production database, which is on SAP PERSON_NAME 2.0 SP05 Rev 55

Source system server is at 780 GB memory and the maximum memory utilized is 500 GB.
Target system server is at 250 GB memory.

Backup size of source system is 198 GB and backup size of target system backup size is at 98 GB.
Do we need to upgrade the target system memory to match the source system, before the system copy is executed.?

Kindly advise.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Topology match for System Copy
entry.content:
Dear customer,

For your concern, I would like to suggest that you refer to the admin guide "Prerequisites for Copying a Database Using Backup and Recovery".
Regarding to the memory and disk size of the source and target system, the target system should have the sufficient disk and memory resource.
Please refer to the admin guide 'Prerequisites for the Target Database'.
"The target database has sufficient disk space and memory.
The target system should have at least the same amount of disk space as the source system."

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Overall cash Position check - Cash flow Analyzer App...
entry.content:
@VenuKudupudi Thanks for your question! Based on the information you provided, I think the problem may have something to do with the bank account derivation and the linking of the G/L account to the house bank account.

To resolve this, I think the first thing to check is whether the G/L account has been correctly linked to the house bank account of the bank account. This step is crucial in ensuring accurate balance display. For more information, see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/ac319d8fa4ea4624b40a58d23e3c4627/579e2d551c03bc06e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US.

If the issue persists, I recommend logging a support incident because troubleshooting without detailed product and system information can be challenging. Our support team will be happy to assist you further.

Was ist der Phemex-Einladungscode?

Der Phemex-Einladungscode "HMXFT5" bietet kontinuierliche Rabatte auf Handelsgebühren und bietet 50 % Rabatt auf Trades. Wenn Sie diesen Code außerdem mit Freunden teilen, können Sie bis zu 8.800 $ an Prämien verdienen. Top-Phemex-Einladungscode für 2024

Der Top-Einladungscode für 2024 ist "HMXFT5". Mit diesem Code sparen Sie kontinuierlich Handelsgebühren und erhalten 50 % Rabatt auf Transaktionen. Wenn Sie diesen Code mit Freunden teilen, können Sie einen Bonus von bis zu 8.800 $ erhalten. Melden Sie sich jetzt mit dem Phemex-Einladungscode "HMXFT5" an, um bis zu 8.800 $ zu erhalten und mit dem Handel an der Phemex-Börse zu beginnen.

Wie finde ich den Phemex-Einladungscode?

Der beste Phemex-Einladungscode ist "HMXFT5". Wenn Sie diesen Code verwenden, erhalten Sie einen Anmeldebonus von 8.800 $. Wenn Sie Ihren Code außerdem mit Freunden teilen, können Sie sich eine Provision von 50 % sichern, sodass Sie bis zu 8.800 $ als Willkommensbonus verdienen können.

Wie verwende ich den Phemex-Einladungscode?

Phemex ist eine globale Handelsplattform, die digitale Vermögenswerte, Derivate und Spot-Handelsoptionen anbietet. Phemex ist für seine innovativen Funktionen bekannt und ermöglicht es Benutzern, problemlos auf verschiedenen Märkten zu handeln. Das Unternehmen ist in über 100 Ländern und Regionen tätig, darunter den USA, Kanada, Australien und der EU. Insbesondere vereinfacht Phemex den Onboarding-Prozess mit minimalen KYC-Anforderungen.

So melden Sie sich mit dem Phemex-Einladungscode "HMXFT5" an:

  1. Klicken Sie auf den Link: [Phemex-Anmeldung](https://phemex.com)
  2. Geben Sie Ihre E-Mail-Adresse ein und wählen Sie ein Passwort.
  3. Stellen Sie sicher, dass der Code "HMXFT5" unter "Einladungscode (optional)" angezeigt wird. Wenn nicht, geben Sie ihn ein.
  4. Stimmen Sie der Kundenvereinbarung und der Datenschutzrichtlinie zu.
    Klicken Sie auf "Anmelden".

Wie wende ich den Phemex-Einladungscode an?

  1. Rufen Sie die Phemex-Website auf oder laden Sie die Phemex-App aus dem Google Play Store oder Apple App Store herunter.
  2. Registrieren Sie sich mit Ihrer E-Mail-Adresse oder Handynummer, legen Sie ein Passwort fest und geben Sie den Einladungscode "HMXFT5" ein.
    Verwenden Sie den Einladungscode "HMXFT5", um bis zu 8.800 $ und 50 % Gebührenrabatt auf den Handel zu erhalten.
  3. Senden Sie Ihre Registrierung ab, um einen Bestätigungscode zu erhalten.
    Nach der Bestätigung erhalten Sie eine Willkommensbonusnachricht und werden zum Bereich „Belohnungen“ weitergeleitet.
  4. Fügen Sie Vermögenswerte aus Ihrem privaten Schließfach oder Bankkonto hinzu und navigieren Sie dann zur Krypto-Seite, um mit dem Handel zu beginnen.
  5. Wählen Sie auf der Haupt-Homepage aus einer Vielzahl von Kryptowährungen aus, in die Sie investieren möchten.
  6. Beobachten Sie den Markt und verkaufen Sie Ihre Währung, wenn ihr Wert steigt, um Gewinne auszuzahlen.

Wie gebe ich den Phemex-Einladungscode weiter?

Geben Sie den Phemex-Einladungscode "HMXFT5" an Freunde und Familie weiter, damit diese auf exklusive Angebote von Phemex Exchange zugreifen können.

entry.title: for using odata api, we need gradle version in place of maven, how to solve it
entry.content:
sap cloud sdk suggested using maven for creating odata api on local development.

but we need gradle version and converted maven version into gradle version

but we failed on "gradle build". how to solve it

- maven version

<plugin>
<groupId>com.sap.cloud.sdk.datamodel</groupId>
<artifactId>odata-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
<version>${cloud-sdk.version}</version>
<executions>
<execution>
<id>generate-consumption</id>
<phase>generate-sources</phase>
<goals>
<goal>generate</goal>
</goals>
<configuration>
<inputDirectory>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/edmx</inputDirectory>
<outputDirectory>${project.build.sourceDirectory}</outputDirectory>
<packageName>com.sap.cloud.sdk.tutorial.datamodel.odata</packageName>
<serviceNameMappingFile>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/serviceNameMappings.properties</serviceNameMappingFile>
<compileScope>COMPILE</compileScope>
<overwriteFiles>true</overwriteFiles>
</configuration>
</execution>
</executions>
</plugin>

- gradle verison

import com.sap.cloud.sdk.datamodel.odata.generator.DataModelGenerator
--> in the part, error occured, "not resolve class"

tasks.register('generateOData') {
    doLast {
        def generator = new com.sap.cloud.sdk.datamodel.odata.generator.DataModelGenerator()

        generator.setInputDirectory(file("${projectDir}/src/main/resources/edmx"))
        generator.setOutputDirectory(file("${projectDir}/src/main"))
        generator.setPackageName("com.sks.erpbss.be.cs.datamodel.odata")
        generator.setServiceNameMappingFile(file("${projectDir}/src/main/resources/serviceNameMappings.properties"))
        generator.setCompileScope(ODataGeneratorPlugin.CompileScope.COMPILE)
        generator.setOverwriteFiles(true)

        generator.generate()
    }
}
compileJava.dependsOn generateOData

entry.title: Re: SAP GROUP REPORTING ADDITIONAL DIMENSION MASTER DATA
entry.content:
Hi Mark,

Segment is a separate field in GR, if you can populate this field in S4 using any substitution rules then it will flow to GR.

Null value in Profit center is accepted in ACDOCU table, as far as I know there is no substitution possible in GR while releasing the data from S4, to GR it can be substituted in case of manual journal entries within GR.

Regards

Arun

entry.title: Is Temu $100 off Coupon Code {act922760} legit
entry.content:
Temu's $100 coupon {act922760} can be a great way to save money on your first purchase, but be sure to approach it with a little caution and do your research before you buy.

For businesses operating in Brazil, navigating the complexities of tax regulations can be a challenge. A crucial aspect of this is complying with electronic invoicing, mandated by the Nota Fiscal Eletronica (NF-e) system. This article explores SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica, a solution designed to simplify NF-e compliance for SAP users.

Understanding NF-e
NF-e refers to the electronic invoice format used in Brazil for transactions involving goods. It eliminates paper invoices, promoting tax efficiency and streamlining the invoicing process. Companies must generate NF-e in a specific XML format, containing details like buyer and seller information, product specifics, and tax data.

SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica: A Helping Hand
SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica is a software solution offered by SAP that aids businesses in complying with NF-e regulations. It integrates seamlessly with existing SAP systems, automating manual tasks and ensuring accurate invoice generation.

Key Benefits of SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica
Enhanced Compliance: The solution automates NF-e generation in the mandated XML format, minimizing errors and ensuring compliance with Brazilian tax authorities (SEFAZ).
Streamlined Processes: Manual data entry for invoices is significantly reduced, saving time and resources.
Improved Accuracy: Automating invoice creation reduces the risk of errors associated with manual data entry.
Secure Communication: Secure and certified communication with SEFAZ is facilitated through the solution.
Efficiency Gains: Faster invoice processing translates to quicker settlements and improved cash flow.
Important Note: Upcoming End of Maintenance"

It's important to be aware that SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica (SAP NFE) is an on-premise solution nearing its end-of-maintenance date (December 31, 2025). SAP recommends exploring alternative solutions to ensure continued compliance with evolving NF-e regulations.

entry.title: Re: Internal Error ocurred while processing
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

This error is very generic and it can occur due to different reasons, which needs to be checked by going through browser network logs and Splunk.

If this is error showing up in Query designer in many cases, we have seen this is showing up for an issue in Calculated columns, if the syntax used in any of the calculated columns is incorrect then this error shows up.

You can test this by removing each calculated column and click on preview query to find out which particular calculated column has an issue.

Regards,

Subbu

entry.title: Re: Unable to fetch Purchase Order Item Note details via...
entry.content:
Thank Sijin for the response. Appreciated your response.

We did some digging and found out that the table ESH_SR_LTXT  from which the CDS view is picking the data from, and further to the API.

The values in the specific table were not being updated/filled.

Further we searched and found out a note: 3048704 - Long text (SAPScript) search enablement for CDS-based search 

Which contains a program: ESH_SR_LTXT_REPLICATE which needs to be run.

We ran the program and could see the table being updated with the long text details.

Hence the API was able to fetch the details and display.

This resolved the issue

entry.title: 21 Generative AI Jargons Simplified: Part 2 — AI Model
entry.content:
This is part of the blog series, where I am explaining 21 frequently used Generative AI jargons in simple words. To make it super simple and interesting, I will provide an analogy as well.

Note: I am publishing it as a series. Each week I will publish one blog explaining one jargon. 3 to 21 are yet to be published.

 

  1. Prompt Engineering
  2. AI Model [Current Blog]
  3. Foundation Model
  4. AI Hallucination
  5. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
  6. Grounding
  7. Natural Language Processing (NLP)
  8. Explainable AI
  9. Prompt Injection Attack
  10. Overfitting and Underfitting
  11. Multimodality
  12. Autoencoders
  13. Computer Vision
  14. Transfer Learning
  15. AI Detectors
  16. Adversarial Attacks
  17. Data Augmentation
  18. Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs)
  19. Variational Autoencoders (VAEs)
  20. Transformer-Based Models
  21. AI Poisoning Attacks

This is 2nd blog in this series. The jargon is AI Model.

Side Note: I strongly recommend you to go through the blog series Generative AI for Beginner. It will only take 90 minutes of your time. No perquisites.

Let’s start!

 

An Analogy to Understand AI Model

Imagine there is a super-smart genie living in your computer. It’s not the blue, wish-granting kind, but more like a program with a knack for crunching numbers and solving puzzles faster than you can say “Abracadabra!”.

There are 2 major power this genie has. First, it can understand and process tons of information. It can process complex calculations or analyze vast amounts of text in seconds.

Second, it keeps learning new things. It gets smarter and more accurate over time with experience.

Similar to the genie, an AI model is actually a computer program that can understand and process tons of information and can be trained on huge data to perform specific tasks.

 

So, what exactly is AI Model?

An AI Model is a computer program that has been trained to perform specific tasks by learning from data. The AI models (programs) usually include complex mathematical and computational techniques to process vast amounts of data and extract meaningful insights.

Below image summarizes important points about AI Model.

 

 

How does a typical AI Model work?

AI Models have 3 major components — Algorithms, Data and Parameters.

Algorithms

AI models uses mathematical formulas and rules that define the model’s behavior and how it processes information.

Data

The data is the foundation of everything an AI model does. There are basically two types of data used in AI models — Training Data and Input Data.

Parameters

AI models have adjustable elements which are fine-tuned during training to optimize its performance.

 

Training of AI model

AI models need huge datasets to get trained. The training data may include text, images, videos, numbers, or any other format of data. Some powerful AI models are trained on entire Internet data.

1. The training data first goes through some cleaning and pre-processing to make sure that it’s consistent and noise-free.

2. The prepared data is then fed to the AI models.

3. The AI model’s algorithm analyzes the training data and search for underlying patterns and relationships between different data points.

4. Based on the analysis, the AI model adjusts its internal parameters to better represent the discovered patterns.

Steps 1, 2 and 3 are repeated over and over again with different set of training data. This helps AI model to become more skilled at recognizing the patterns.

 

Examples of AI Model

Some of the popular examples of AI models are:

GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer)

Developed by OpenAI, GPT is a language model. It’s the AI model behind ChatGPT.

BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers)

Developed by Google, BERT is a powerful language model designed to understand context and nuances in language. It’s widely used for natural language processing tasks.

AlphaGo

Developed by DeepMind, AlphaGo is an AI model that have mastered the game of Go and achieved superhuman performance. AlphaGo defeated human champions.

 

Let’s Create a Simple AI Model

We can build a simple AI model using Python. In this example, we will create a basic AI model to identifying objects in images.

Expected Output

An Image Recognition Model to identifying objects in images (e.g., cats and dogs).

Note — If you are new to Python, don’t worry. Just complete this 2-minute crash course on Python.

Follow below steps:

 

Step 1 — Install Necessary Libraries

Run below command to install TensorFlow library

pip install tensorflow

 

Step 2 — Write below Python code to create the AI model. This code snippet trains a basic image recognition model on the CIFAR-10 dataset and then makes a prediction on a new image.

# Import Libraries: Import TensorFlow and other necessary libraries.
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Dense, Flatten, Conv2D, MaxPooling2D
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import cifar10

 

# Load and preprocess the CIFAR-10 dataset
# CIFAR-10 dataset contains 60,000 color images in 10 classes (e.g., airplanes, cars, birds).
(x_train, y_train), (x_test, y_test) = cifar10.load_data()


# Preprocess Data: Normalize the image data by dividing by 255.0.
x_train, x_test = x_train / 255.0, x_test / 255.0


# Define the Model: Create a simple convolutional neural network (CNN),
# with layers for convolution, pooling, flattening, and dense layers.
model = Sequential([
Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=(32, 32, 3)),
MaxPooling2D((2, 2)),
Flatten(),
Dense(64, activation='relu'),
Dense(10, activation='softmax') # 10 classes in CIFAR-10
])


# Compile the Model.
# Configure for training by specifying optimizer, loss function, and metrics.
model.compile(optimizer='adam',
loss='sparse_categorical_crossentropy',
metrics=['accuracy'])


# Train the Model on the training data
model.fit(x_train, y_train, epochs=10, validation_data=(x_test, y_test))


# Make predictions on test data
predictions = model.predict(x_test)


# Output the prediction for the first test image
predicted_class = tf.argmax(predictions[0])
print(f"Predicted class: {predicted_class}")

 

This AI model can further be used in variety of use-cases, for example in a smartphone app that identifies the breed of a dog when you take a picture of it.

 

Next Blog

21 Generative AI Jargons Simplified: 3 — Foundation Model [To be published]

entry.title: Re: Unable to fetch Purchase Order Item Note details via...
entry.content:
We did some digging and found out that the table ESH_SR_LTXT  from which the CDS view is picking the data from, and further to the API.

The values in the specific table were not being updated/filled.

Note : 3048704 - Long text (SAPScript) search enablement for CDS-based search 

Which contains a program: ESH_SR_LTXT_REPLICATE which needs to be run.

We ran the program and could see the table being updated with the long text details.

Hence the API was able to fetch the details and display.

This resolved the issue

entry.title: Re: How to Download Complete Message Class ?
entry.content:
Hi,

Go to T-code SE11 or SE16N.

Table - T100

Then go to contents.

Put your class name (standard or custom) in the ARBGB area. If you received the message class number, enter it in the MSGNR field.

Here you will get the details of your message class.

entry.content:

Is it possible to run the Readiness Check 2.0 in a system, where the SAP_APPL component is not installed?

Best regards,
Customer 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Run the Readiness Check 2.0 without SAP_APPL componen...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

A system conversion to SAP S/4HANA is only possible for a SAP ERP system (e.g. a full stack ERP installation where all ERP components like SAP-APPL, EA-APPL etc. are installed).

For a standard Netweaver, Business Suite Foundation system or others without SAP-APPL, a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA is technically not possible and not supported. Hence also Readiness Check is not applicable to this system.

The transition path to SAP S/4HANA for such a system would be to take a greenfield approach and migrate any required E-Recruiting data into an already existing or newly installed SAP S/4HANA system.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: OS upgrade SAP ECC Suite on HANA
entry.content:
Dear Support,

We are planning a technological renewal of our operating system infrastructure.


Our SAP system is SAP ECC on NW 7.4 SP16 with PERSON_NAME DB version 2.00.059.PHONE_NUMBER
We are planning an upgrade from RHEL 7.x to 9.x - with clustering of ASCS and ERS resources.
We also have two application servers (PAS and AAS) on different server.


Following this guide:

 https://access.redhat.com/articles/3569681#overview to realize ASCS/ERS cluster.

This is the question: based on above guide (paragraph 2.3) PAS and AAS are installed on separated servers besides of ASCS/ERS cluster servers, we ask you to confirm that we can install PAS and AAS on the same servers of ASCS/ERS cluster.Please we kindly request your feedback


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: OS upgrade SAP ECC Suite on HANA
entry.content:
Yes, you can install PAS (Primary Application Server) and AAS (Additional Application Server) on the same servers as ASCS/ERS cluster. However, it's important to note that this configuration may not be optimal for performance and high availability.

The ASCS (ABAP SAP Central Services) and ERS (Enqueue Replication Server) are critical components of the SAP system, and they are usually installed on separate servers to ensure high availability and performance. If you install the PAS and AAS on the same servers, they will compete for resources with the ASCS and ERS, which could potentially impact the performance and availability of your SAP system.

Therefore, while it's technically possible to install PAS and AAS on the same servers as ASCS/ERS cluster, it's generally recommended to install them on separate servers for optimal performance and high availability.

However, your specific requirements and constraints may dictate otherwise. If you have limited hardware resources, or if your SAP system is not heavily used, then installing PAS and AAS on the same servers as ASCS/ERS cluster might be a viable option.

In any case, you should carefully evaluate your specific needs and constraints before making a decision.

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Outlook (version 2405) rendering issues

this is definitely an issue with outlook! The preview within SMC is not an email client and is just displaying the html which is created by the editor. In the past the most issues we experienced was always outlook because each email client interprets the email-HTML differently and outlook has it's own way :(, even each outlook version does this too.

Many issues comes also from custom CSS, if there definition which outlook does not support or ignores. You can solve issue when using inline CSS instead of CSS in the HTML header (custom CSS).

You always can open a customer incident so that we are able to analyze your email we causes your described issue.

I hope I could help you a bit.

Best Regards
Ralf Freyler
SAP Marketing Cloud Development

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Business Application Studio – June Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we cross the halfway mark of the year, it’s the perfect time to reflect on our progress and introduce the exciting new features we've added over the past few months.
Here’s what’s new:

 

Enhancing SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment

Following up on Franziska's March update, we're excited to expand the capabilities of SAPUI5 adaptation projects. Now, you can unlock the full potential of SAP applications in SAP BTP, ABAP environment: We've enhanced the SAPUI5 flexibility to allow developers to customize standard SAP apps to meet specific customer requirements. For detailed guidance, check out our documentation on leveraging SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in the SAP Business Application Studio.

Image 1: Template Wizard in SAP Business Application with SAPUI5 Adaptation Project Tile

 

Updated HDI Deployer in SAP HANA Projects

We’ve upgraded the SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure Deployer (HDI deployer) in the “SAP HANA Database Project” template from version 4 to 5. This new version streamlines project setups by removing the built-in SAP HANA client, reducing overhead and simplifying client management.

While the generation of new projects has already been adjusted, if you're upgrading existing projects, make sure to manually include the SAP HANA client in your project dependencies to avoid disruptions.

Image 2: Manual insertion of SAP HANA Client in existing project

To assist you with the transition, SAP Business Application Studio now includes a problem indicator for projects using HDI deployer version 5 without an SAP HANA client. This problem indicator not only helps to discover incomplete configurations it also offers a quick-fix action for easy resolution.

Image 3: Problem Indicator for SAP HANA client dependency in SAP Business Application Studio

We encourage all developers to upgrade to this latest version to benefit from the newest features and innovations.

 

Discover how to extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Code

Have you heard about SAP Build Code? It’s our latest offering on SAP BTP, tailored for developers working with SAPUI5, SAP Fiori elements and CAP. It offers a turn-key development environment that integrates runtime and design time capabilities, facilitating coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. SAP Build Code includes Joule, a generative AI copilot, enhancing developer productivity by automating the generation of application logic, data models, and testing scripts, allowing for a smoother and faster development process. 

Discover more about this innovative solution by watching this video (https://youtu.be/py5AST8UGe4?si=LgjWBVWJ1g8KvSbF) or dive deeper into transitioning from the SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code in this insightful blog post by Rupa Maity.

 

Influence SAP products and join our SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives

  • SAP S/4HANA Extensibility using SAP Build, SAP Build Code and ABAP
    Shape the future of enterprise applications with the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for SAP Build and SAP Build Code. This initiative offers a unique opportunity to collaborate on developing Extension Scenarios and Extension Applications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud systems in Side-by-Side mode. By participating, you’ll engage directly with the latest SAP technologies for  Process Extensions, Application Extensions, Mobile Extensions, and ABAP-based Extensions, as well as advanced Cloud API consumption scenarios. Help us tailor these tools to your real-life business needs and use cases, ensuring they serve you better in driving efficient, integrated development across your enterprise environments.
  • SAPUI5 2.x
    Are you looking to adapt your app to SAPUI5 2.x? Join our upcoming Customer Engagement Initiative project and influence how applications evolve with SAPUI5 2.x. For more details and to register, read this blog post by Oliver Graeff and make your mark!

 

For further information on new features, please have a look at the “What’s new” information for SAP Business Application Studio and check our roadmap. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding ! 😊
